steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with "I have less need now than when I was young" — a past/present comparison. The underlined portion must logically follow as examples of what the speaker needed in youth but no longer needs now.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It explains the speaker's views about proper conduct toward new acquaintances.: reframes the underlined content as a general observation about people, ignoring the speaker's personal past/present contrast. ✗
  • Choice B — It provides examples of behaviors the speaker is less inclined toward than she once was.: correctly identifies the two behaviors — sharing herself with every comer, shaping thoughts into words — as what she needed when young but no longer needs, directly fulfilling the comparison the opening sets up. ✓
  • Choice C — It presents an episode from the speaker's youth that she remembers fondly.: treats the content as a recalled episode rather than illustrations of a continuing contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — It emphasizes the social habits the speaker has developed over her lifetime.: misreads the behaviors as current habits rather than past ones the speaker has moved beyond. ✗
That matches Choice B — It provides examples of behaviors the speaker is less inclined toward than she once was..
